title: Phegopteris holophylla
taxonomy: Phegopteris holophylla is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Phegopteris, which is a part of the phylum Pteridophyta. The classification of Phegopteris holophylla can also be broken down to the order Polypodiales and class Polypodiopsida. Additionally, it is a vascular plant and an epiphyte, which means it grows on other plants, such as trees. Phegopteris holophylla is native to Central and South America.

preparations: Phegopteris holophylla, also known as the maidenhair fern, is a plant species traditionally used in herbal preparations. intro: Phegopteris holophylla, also known as the Maidenhair fern, is a species of vascular plant in the family Thelypteridaceae. The health benefits of Phegopteris holophylla include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Phegopteris holophylla is commonly used in traditional medicine to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote overall health and well-being.
